rulings.Case Title: JOEL TATE JR ET AL VS. A. ELAINE MARTIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2018, Line 2. PLAINTIFFS JOEL TATE, DAISY TATE'S Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement. GRANTED as follows: Linton Martin is ordered to execute a Quitclaim Deed within 15 days of notice of entry order. If Linton Martin fails to comply, judgment shall be entered in favor of Plaintiff and against Linton Martin and A. Elaine Martin. Attorney's fees granted in the amount of $3,150 against Linton Martin to be paid within 15 days of notice of entry of order. Plaintiff may appear ex-parte to obtain judgment upon expiration of 15 days and proper notice. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RALPH MENDELSON ET AL VS. ROGER FORBES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2018, Line 3. Order To Show Cause. Having considered written submissions the Court finds no contempt. 1. The Court notes Defendants technically violated non-mandatory provisions of the Court's injunction order by untimely complying with arguably mandatory provisions of the Order. Defendants failed to cease construction related activities after June 2, 2018. Defendants' admit that construction-related activity, removal of the scaffolding, continued through June 14, 2018. 2. The Court also notes that Defendants' counsel represented to the Court on April 24, 2018 in written response to Preliminary Injunction request that constriction would be complete within one month. At the hearing on May 21, 2018 the Court inquired if the date of completion was a mere speculation and also asked several times about the date of completion by "end of June." Counsel responded that there was no speculation and that the construction will be complete "by June." Counsel was given an opportunity to revise his timeline, but he chose not to instead he mentioned the uncertainties inherent in construction business. Completion date was in fact a speculative moving target and all present at the hearing are aware of it. 3. While arguments regarding the date of completion were made and technical violation of the order took place, this does not rise to the level of contempt. =(501/REQ)
Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2018, Line 4. DEFENDANT SUSAN PLICHCIK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. OFF CALENDAR, First Amended Complaint on file. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ED LOCKETT VS. EFREN SANCHEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave Of Court To Amend Answer; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration In Support Thereof

DEFENDANTS EFREN SANCHEZ, JUAN ARIAS, GABRIELA ARIAS' Motion For Leave Of Court To Amend Answer. Granted, subject to Defendant filing and presenting proof of service for executed Order Shortening Time. First Amended Answer to be filed forthwith. No opposition filed. This motion will be heard at 9:30 a.m. on June 27, 2018 in department 502 by the Honorable A. James Robertson. =(501/AJR)
